#!/usr/bin/env luajit

package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;" .. package.path

local ffi = require("ffi")
local Facts = require("src.facts")
local SSA = require("src.ssa")
local StencilLower = require("src.stencil_lower")
local Materialize = require("src.materialize_native_x64")

ffi.cdef[[
typedef struct SponTValueABI {
  unsigned long long value_;
  unsigned char tt_;
} SponTValueABI;
typedef struct SponExecCtx {
  void *stack;
  SponTValueABI *k;
  SponTValueABI scratch[256];
  uint32_t exit_kind;
  uint32_t exit_pc;
  uint32_t exit_op_idx;
  uint32_t exit_hole;
} SponExecCtx;
]]

local function assert_eq(a, b, msg) if a ~= b then error((msg or "assert_eq failed") .. ": " .. tostring(a) .. " ~= " .. tostring(b), 2) end end
local function assert_true(x, msg) if not x then error(msg or "assert_true failed", 2) end end
local function assert_false(x, msg) if x then error(msg or "assert_false failed", 2) end end

local function lower(ops, facts)
  local r = SSA.compile(ops, facts or {})
  assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local fr = StencilLower.generate(r, { facts = facts or {} })
  assert_true(fr.ok, table.concat(fr.errors or {}, "\n"))
  assert_true(fr.stencil.executable == true and fr.stencil.exact == true and (fr.stencil.code_size or 0) > 0, "exact native stencil expected")
  return fr.stencil
end

local function has_fallthrough(f)
  for _, cr in ipairs(f.control_relocs or {}) do
    if cr.edge_kind == "fallthrough" and cr.reloc_kind == "pc32" then return true end
  end
  return false
end

local function run_image(img, setup, check)
  local mem, n = assert(Materialize.alloc_executable(img.bytes))
  local fn = ffi.cast("void (*)(SponExecCtx *)", mem)
  local stack = ffi.new("SponTValueABI[8]")
  local k = ffi.new("SponTValueABI[8]")
  local ctx = ffi.new("SponExecCtx")
  ctx.stack = stack
  ctx.k = k
  setup(ctx, stack, k)
  fn(ctx)
  check(ctx, stack, k)
  Materialize.free_executable(mem, n)
end

-- Two native ADDI stencils are copied into one image. The first success edge is
-- patched from its local ok stub to the second stencil body; the second success
-- edge keeps its local ok stub.
do
  local facts = { Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R0"), "is_i64", true) }
  local ops1 = { { op = "ADDI", a = 0, b = 0, c = 7 } }
  local ops2 = { { op = "ADDI", a = 0, b = 0, c = 8 } }
  local f1 = lower(ops1, facts)
  local f2 = lower(ops2, facts)
  assert_true(has_fallthrough(f1), "first stencil missing explicit fallthrough reloc")
  assert_true(has_fallthrough(f2), "second stencil missing explicit fallthrough reloc")
  local img = Materialize.materialize({ { stencil = f1, ops = ops1 }, { stencil = f2, ops = ops2 } })
  assert_true(img.ok and img.code_size == f1.code_size + f2.code_size, "linked image size")

  run_image(img,
    function(ctx, stack)
      stack[0].value_ = 10
      stack[0].tt_ = 3 -- LUA_VNUMINT
      ctx.exit_kind = 99
    end,
    function(ctx, stack)
      assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_kind), 0, "linked ADDI exit")
      assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].value_), 25, "linked ADDI result")
      assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].tt_), 3, "linked ADDI tag")
    end)

  run_image(img,
    function(ctx, stack)
      stack[0].value_ = 10
      stack[0].tt_ = 0 -- guard fails in first stencil; second must not run
      ctx.exit_kind = 99
    end,
    function(ctx, stack)
      assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_kind), 1, "linked ADDI guard exit")
      assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].value_), 10, "guard failure must not mutate slot")
    end)
end

-- Const-pool relocation is patched from actual site operands, then fallthrough
-- links into an i64 ADDI stencil.
do
  local load_ops = { { op = "LOADK", a = 0, b = 2 } }
  local add_ops = { { op = "ADDI", a = 0, b = 0, c = 5 } }
  local f_load = lower(load_ops, {})
  local f_add = lower(add_ops, { Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R0"), "is_i64", true) })
  local img = Materialize.materialize({ { stencil = f_load, ops = load_ops }, { stencil = f_add, ops = add_ops } })

  run_image(img,
    function(ctx, stack, k)
      k[2].value_ = 37
      k[2].tt_ = 3
      stack[0].value_ = 0
      stack[0].tt_ = 0
      ctx.exit_kind = 99
    end,
    function(ctx, stack)
      assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_kind), 0, "LOADK->ADDI exit")
      assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].value_), 42, "LOADK->ADDI result")
      assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].tt_), 3, "LOADK->ADDI tag")
    end)
end

do
  local f = lower({ { op = "LOADTRUE", a = 0 } }, {})
  f.exact = false
  f.abi.stencil.flags = 2
  local ok = pcall(function() Materialize.materialize({ { stencil = f, ops = { { op = "LOADTRUE", a = 0 } } } }) end)
  assert_false(ok, "materializer must reject non-exact native stencils")
end

print("ok - SpongeJIT native materializer links stencils")

#!/usr/bin/env luajit

package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;" .. package.path

local Facts = require("src.facts")
local SSA = require("src.ssa")
local StencilLower = require("src.stencil_lower")

local function assert_eq(a, b, msg) if a ~= b then error((msg or "assert_eq failed") .. ": " .. tostring(a) .. " ~= " .. tostring(b), 2) end end
local function assert_true(x, msg) if not x then error(msg or "assert_true failed", 2) end end
local function assert_false(x, msg) if x then error(msg or "assert_false failed", 2) end end

local function has_endpoint(f, kind)
  for _, ep in ipairs(f.endpoints or {}) do if ep.kind == kind then return true, ep end end
  return false
end

local function has_data_role(f, role)
  for _, r in ipairs(f.data_relocs or {}) do if r.role_kind == role then return true end end
  return false
end

local function has_control(f, kind)
  for _, r in ipairs(f.control_relocs or {}) do if r.edge_kind == kind then return true, r end end
  return false
end

local function has_error(errors, needle)
  for _, e in ipairs(errors or {}) do if tostring(e):find(needle, 1, true) then return true end end
  return false
end

local function assert_exit_projection(f, endpoint_kind, control_kind)
  local ok_ep, ep = has_endpoint(f, endpoint_kind)
  assert_true(ok_ep, endpoint_kind .. " endpoint missing")
  assert_true(ep.projection_start and f.projections[ep.projection_start], endpoint_kind .. " projection range missing")
  assert_true((ep.n_projections or 0) > 0, endpoint_kind .. " projection count missing")
  assert_true(has_control(f, control_kind), control_kind .. " control reloc missing")
end

-- End-to-end conforming i64 slot arithmetic stencil descriptor.
do
  local facts = { Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R1"), "is_i64", true) }
  local r = SSA.compile({ { op = "ADDI", a = 1, b = 1, c = 1 } }, facts)
  assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local fr = StencilLower.generate(r, { facts = facts })
  assert_true(fr.ok, table.concat(fr.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local f = fr.stencil
  assert_eq(f.physical_abi, "x86_64_sysv_spon_v1", "physical ABI")
  assert_eq(f.abi.stencil.physical_abi, 1, "ABI-lowered physical ABI")
  assert_true(f.exact == true, "native stencil must be exact")
  assert_eq(f.abi.stencil.flags, 10, "native exact stencil flags")
  assert_true(f.executable == true and f.layout.mode == "native_bytes", "stencil must carry executable native bytes")
  assert_true(type(f.code_hex) == "string" and #f.code_hex > 0, "native code bytes missing")
  assert_true((f.code_size or 0) > 0 and f.abi.stencil.size == f.code_size, "native code size missing")
  assert_true(has_endpoint(f, "entry"), "entry endpoint missing")
  assert_true(has_endpoint(f, "ok"), "ok endpoint missing")
  assert_true(has_endpoint(f, "guard_exit"), "guard exit endpoint missing")
  for _, ep in ipairs(f.endpoints or {}) do assert_true(#(ep.locations or {}) > 0, "endpoint locations must be explicit") end
  assert_true(#(f.clobbers or {}) > 0, "stencil clobbers must be explicit")
  assert_true(has_data_role(f, "slot"), "slot data reloc missing")
  assert_true(has_data_role(f, "slot_store"), "slot_store data reloc missing")
  assert_true(has_data_role(f, "imm"), "imm data reloc missing")
  assert_true(has_control(f, "guard_fail"), "guard_fail control reloc missing")
  for _, dr in ipairs(f.data_relocs or {}) do
    assert_true(dr.role_kind ~= "fail" and dr.role_kind ~= "exit", "exit/fail must not be data relocs")
    assert_true(dr.code_offset_kind ~= "abstract_zero", "native data reloc must have real offset marker")
    assert_true(dr.reloc_kind == "abs32s" or dr.reloc_kind == "abs64" or dr.reloc_kind == "abs8", "native data reloc encoding missing")
  end
  for _, cr in ipairs(f.control_relocs or {}) do
    assert_true(cr.code_offset_kind ~= "abstract_zero", "native control reloc must have real offset marker")
    assert_eq(cr.reloc_kind, "pc32", "native control reloc encoding")
  end
  assert_true(f.fact_transfer.checked_sig, "checked signature missing")
  assert_true(f.fact_transfer.produced_sig, "produced signature missing")
  assert_true(f.fact_transfer.killed_sig, "killed signature missing")
end

-- Mixed unary/binary integer loop body lowers as one native byte stencil. This
-- keeps the descriptor path from regressing to a metadata-only shape.
do
  local facts = {
    Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R0"), "is_i64", true),
    Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R1"), "is_i64", true),
    Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R3"), "is_i64", true),
  }
  local r = SSA.compile({
    { op = "UNM", a = 1, b = 0 },
    { op = "MUL", a = 3, b = 3, c = 3 },
  }, facts)
  assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local fr = StencilLower.generate(r, { facts = facts })
  assert_true(fr.ok, table.concat(fr.errors or {}, "\n"))
  assert_eq(fr.stencil.len, 2, "mixed stencil length")
  assert_true(#(fr.stencil.slotmaps or {}) >= 4, "mixed stencil slotmaps")
  assert_true(has_control(fr.stencil, "guard_fail"), "mixed stencil guard control reloc")
end

-- Boundary exit lowering is covered and projected.
do
  local r = SSA.compile({ { op = "RETURN1", a = 1 } }, {})
  assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local fr = StencilLower.generate(r, {})
  assert_true(fr.ok, table.concat(fr.errors or {}, "\n"))
  assert_true(fr.stencil.exact == true, "boundary stencil must be exact")
  assert_exit_projection(fr.stencil, "boundary_exit", "boundary")
end

-- Unsupported opcode/fact cases reject before descriptor lowering.
do
  local r = SSA.compile({ { op = "POW", a = 1, b = 1, c = 1 } }, {})
  assert_false(r.ok, "POW without exact lowering must reject")
  assert_true(has_error(r.errors, "opcode_not_supported") or has_error(r.errors, "not_semantically_lowered"), table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))

  for _, bop in ipairs({
    { op = "LFALSESKIP", a = 1 },
    { op = "MMBIN", a = 0, b = 1, c = 0 },
    { op = "LEN", a = 0, b = 1 },
    { op = "CONCAT", a = 0, b = 0, c = 2 },
    { op = "CLOSE", a = 0 },
    { op = "TBC", a = 0 },
    { op = "NEWTABLE", a = 0, b = 0, c = 0 },
    { op = "SETLIST", a = 0, b = 0, c = 0 },
    { op = "CLOSURE", a = 0, bx = 0 },
    { op = "VARARG", a = 0, c = 0 },
    { op = "GETVARG", a = 0, c = 0 },
    { op = "ERRNNIL", a = 0 },
    { op = "VARARGPREP", a = 0 },
    { op = "EXTRAARG", ax = 0 },
  }) do
    local br = SSA.compile({ bop }, {})
    assert_true(br.ok, bop.op .. ": " .. table.concat(br.errors or {}, "\n"))
    local bfr = StencilLower.generate(br, {})
    assert_true(bfr.ok, bop.op .. ": " .. table.concat(bfr.errors or {}, "\n"))
    assert_eq(bfr.stencil.semantic_mode, "boundary", bop.op .. " must be exact VM boundary")
    assert_exit_projection(bfr.stencil, "boundary_exit", "boundary")
  end

  local unknown = SSA.compile_nodes({ { op = "MysteryNativeOp", source = 3, inputs = {}, outputs = {}, effect = "none" } }, {})
  assert_false(unknown.ok, "unknown reopened node must reject")
end

-- Fact-specialized raw field stencils lower only with explicit payload facts.
do
  local weak = SSA.compile({ { op = "GETFIELD", a = 1, b = 0, c = 2 } }, {
    Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R0"), "is_table", true),
    Facts.fact("shape", Facts.slot("R0"), "shape_known", true),
    Facts.fact("metatable", Facts.slot("R0"), "metatable_absent", true),
    Facts.fact("constant", Facts.value("K2"), "key_const", true),
  })
  assert_false(weak.ok, "GETFIELD without shape/field payload facts must reject")
  assert_true(has_error(weak.errors, "missing_shape_payload_fact"), table.concat(weak.errors or {}, "\n"))

  local facts = {
    Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R0"), "is_table", true),
    Facts.fact("shape", Facts.slot("R0"), "shape_eq", "shape:test"),
    Facts.fact("metatable", Facts.slot("R0"), "metatable_absent", true),
    Facts.fact("constant", Facts.value("K2"), "key_const", true),
    Facts.fact("shape", Facts.value("K2"), "field_offset", "shape:test"),
  }
  local r = SSA.compile({ { op = "GETFIELD", a = 1, b = 0, c = 2 } }, facts)
  assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local fr = StencilLower.generate(r, { facts = facts })
  assert_true(fr.ok, table.concat(fr.errors or {}, "\n"))
  assert_true(has_data_role(fr.stencil, "shape_offset"), "GETFIELD shape_offset reloc missing")
  assert_true(has_data_role(fr.stencil, "shape_id"), "GETFIELD shape_id reloc missing")
  assert_true(has_data_role(fr.stencil, "metatable_offset"), "GETFIELD metatable_offset reloc missing")
  assert_true(has_data_role(fr.stencil, "field_offset"), "GETFIELD field_offset reloc missing")
  assert_true(has_control(fr.stencil, "guard_fail"), "GETFIELD guard relocs missing")
end

print("ok - SpongeJIT stencil to native stencil")

#!/usr/bin/env luajit
-- Real SponJIT fact + SSA invariants.

package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;" .. package.path

local Facts = require("src.facts")
local SSA = require("src.ssa")
local Contract = require("src.ssa_contract")
local Enum = require("src.enumerate")
local FactAxes = require("src.ssa_fact_axes")

local function assert_eq(a, b, msg)
    if a ~= b then error((msg or "assert_eq failed") .. ": " .. tostring(a) .. " ~= " .. tostring(b), 2) end
end

local function assert_true(x, msg)
    if not x then error(msg or "assert_true failed", 2) end
end

local function has(xs, x)
    for _, v in ipairs(xs or {}) do if v == x then return true end end
    return false
end

local function field_facts(include_barrier)
    local xs = {
        Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R0"), "is_table", true),
        Facts.fact("shape", Facts.slot("R0"), "shape_eq", "shape:test"),
        Facts.fact("metatable", Facts.slot("R0"), "metatable_absent", true),
        Facts.fact("constant", Facts.value("K2"), "key_const", true),
        Facts.fact("shape", Facts.value("K2"), "field_offset", "shape:test"),
    }
    if include_barrier then xs[#xs + 1] = Facts.fact("gc", Facts.global_subject(), "barrier_clean", true) end
    return xs
end

-- Fact closure: shape_known implies table.
do
    local fs = Facts.new({ "shape_known" })
    assert_true(fs:implies("shape_known", Facts.value("table")), "shape_known absent")
    assert_true(fs:implies("is_table", Facts.value("table")), "shape_known must imply table")
    assert_true(has(fs:deps(), "shape_epoch"), "shape fact must carry shape_epoch dependency")
end

-- Fact contradiction: one subject cannot have two concrete incompatible types.
do
    local fs = Facts.new({
        Facts.fact("type", Facts.value("x"), "is_i64"),
        Facts.fact("type", Facts.value("x"), "is_table"),
    })
    assert_true(not fs:ok(), "conflicting type facts must be rejected")
end

-- Guards are facts with exits and deps.
do
    local r = SSA.compile({ { op = "GETFIELD", a = 1, b = 0, c = 2 } }, field_facts(false))
    assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
    assert_true(has(r.stencil_ops, "guard_shape"), "shape guard missing")
    assert_true(has(r.deps, "shape_epoch"), "shape dep missing")
    assert_true((r.projection.exit_obligations or 0) >= 3, "guards must project exits")
end

-- compile_nodes reopens typed semantic node specs; raw codegen-op reopening was removed.
do
    local base = SSA.compile({ { op = "ADDI", a = 1, b = 1, c = 1 } }, { Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R1"), "is_i64", true) })
    local saw_mem = false
    local saw_residency = false
    local saw_exit = false
    for _, spec in ipairs(base.active_node_specs or {}) do
        if spec.source ~= nil then assert_true(type(spec.source) == "number", "source must serialize as number") end
        if spec.mem_in and spec.mem_in.frame then saw_mem = true end
        for _, rloc in ipairs(spec.output_residencies or {}) do
            if rloc == "gpr0" then saw_residency = true end
        end
        if spec.exit and spec.exit.reason then saw_exit = true end
    end
    assert_true(saw_mem, "active_node_specs must preserve memory input tokens")
    assert_true(saw_residency, "active_node_specs must preserve value residency")
    assert_true(saw_exit, "active_node_specs must preserve exit objects")

    local r = SSA.compile_nodes(base.active_node_specs, {})
    assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
    assert_true(has(r.stencil_ops, "store_i64_slot"), "typed node reopening should preserve lowered integer store")
    local saw_reopened_residency = false
    local saw_reopened_mem_in = false
    local saw_reopened_mem_out = false
    local saw_reopened_exit = false
    for _, spec in ipairs(r.active_node_specs or {}) do
        if spec.mem_in and spec.mem_in.frame then saw_reopened_mem_in = true end
        if spec.mem_out and spec.mem_out.frame then saw_reopened_mem_out = true end
        if spec.exit and spec.exit.reason then saw_reopened_exit = true end
        for _, rloc in ipairs(spec.output_residencies or {}) do
            if rloc == "gpr0" then saw_reopened_residency = true end
        end
    end
    assert_true(saw_reopened_residency, "compile_nodes must round-trip residency")
    assert_true(saw_reopened_mem_in, "compile_nodes must round-trip memory input tokens")
    assert_true(saw_reopened_mem_out, "compile_nodes must round-trip memory output tokens")
    assert_true(saw_reopened_exit, "compile_nodes must round-trip exit objects")
end

-- Barrier elimination requires a real GC fact.
do
    local no_fact = SSA.compile({ { op = "SETFIELD", a = 0, b = 2, c = 1 } }, field_facts(false))
    local with_fact = SSA.compile({ { op = "SETFIELD", a = 0, b = 2, c = 1 } }, field_facts(true))
    assert_true(not no_fact.ok and (no_fact.rejections or {})[1].reason == "missing_barrier_clean_fact", "field stores require an explicit clean-barrier fact")
    assert_true(with_fact.ok, table.concat(with_fact.errors or {}, "\n"))
    assert_true(not has(with_fact.stencil_ops, "barrier_check"), "barrier_clean should remove barrier")
end

-- Operand-specific facts attach to real PUC slots and drive the same optimization.
do
    local ops = {
        { op = "GETFIELD", a = 1, b = 0, c = 2 },
        { op = "ADDI", a = 1, b = 1, c = 1 },
        { op = "SETFIELD", a = 0, b = 2, c = 1 },
    }
    local facts = {
        Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R0"), "is_table"),
        Facts.fact("shape", Facts.slot("R0"), "shape_eq", "shape:test"),
        Facts.fact("metatable", Facts.slot("R0"), "metatable_absent", true),
        Facts.fact("constant", Facts.value("K2"), "key_const", true),
        Facts.fact("shape", Facts.value("K2"), "field_offset", "shape:test"),
        Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R1"), "is_i64"),
        Facts.fact("gc", Facts.global_subject(), "barrier_clean", true),
    }
    local r = SSA.compile(ops, facts)
    assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
    assert_eq(table.concat(r.stencil_form, "|"), "FIELD_ADDI_UPDATE", "operand-specific field update should fuse semantically")
    assert_true(has(r.checked_facts, "shape_known"), "slot-specific shape fact should be guarded")
end

-- SSA contracts carry real fact lifetime: selector facts checked by tile guards
-- become success-edge facts, writes kill prior slot leases, and stores produce
-- fresh slot facts only when the SSA proves the stored value.
do
    local facts = { Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R1"), "is_i64", true) }
    local r = SSA.compile({ { op = "ADDI", a = 1, b = 1, c = 1 } }, facts)
    local c = Contract.from_result(r, facts)
    assert_eq(c.selector_sig.literal, "0x0000000000000001ULL", "selector should include observed canonical S0:i64")
    assert_eq(c.required_sig.literal, "0x0000000000000000ULL", "guarded i64 is checked, not blindly required")
    assert_eq(c.checked_sig.literal, "0x0000000000000001ULL", "ADDI guard establishes canonical S0:i64 on success")
    assert_eq(c.produced_sig.literal, "0x0000000000000001ULL", "ADDI store produces fresh canonical S0:i64")
    assert_true(c.killed_sig.literal ~= "0x0000000000000000ULL", "ADDI store must kill previous R1 leases")
end

-- Enumerator derives typed fact axes from instruction operands.
do
    local axes = Enum.fact_axes_for_ops({ { op = "ADDI", a = 3, b = 2, c = 1 } })
    local found = false
    for _, f in ipairs(axes) do
        if type(f) == "table" and f.predicate == "is_i64" and Facts.subject_key(f.subject) == "slot:R2" then found = true end
    end
    assert_true(found, "ADDI operand B must produce slot:R2 is_i64 fact axis")
end

-- Loop opcodes are exact VM boundary handoffs until paired loop-region lowering
-- exists; fact axes must not advertise an i64 loop specialization that no
-- lowering consumes.
do
    local numeric_axes = FactAxes.axes_for_ops({ { op = "FORPREP", a = 0, bx = 3 }, { op = "FORLOOP", a = 0, bx = 3 } })
    assert_eq(#numeric_axes, 0, "FOR* must not produce scalar fact axes before loop-region lowering")
    assert_eq(#(numeric_axes.bundles or {}), 0, "FOR* must not produce loop fact bundles before loop-region lowering")
    assert_eq(#Enum.fact_axes_for_ops({ { op = "FORLOOP", a = 0, bx = 3 } }), 0, "corpus loop facts must not imply native loop lowering")

    local prep = SSA.compile({ { op = "FORPREP", a = 0, bx = 3 } }, {})
    assert_true(prep.ok, table.concat(prep.errors or {}, "\n"))
    local prep_node = prep.graph:active_nodes()[1]
    assert_eq(prep_node.op, "Boundary", "FORPREP remains a VM boundary")
    assert_eq(prep_node.args.reason, "numeric_for_region_boundary", "FORPREP boundary reason")

    local tfor = SSA.compile({ { op = "TFORCALL", a = 0 } }, {})
    assert_true(tfor.ok, table.concat(tfor.errors or {}, "\n"))
    local tfor_node = tfor.graph:active_nodes()[1]
    assert_eq(tfor_node.op, "Boundary", "TFORCALL remains a VM boundary")
    assert_eq(tfor_node.args.reason, "generic_for_region_boundary", "TFORCALL boundary reason")
end

-- Unsupported opcode/fact combinations are structured compile failures, not runtime fallback stubs.
do
    local bad = SSA.compile({ { op = "ADDI", a = 1, b = 1, c = 1 } }, {})
    assert_true(not bad.ok, "ADDI without i64 facts must reject")
    assert_eq((bad.rejections or {})[1].reason, "missing_lhs_i64_fact", "ADDI rejection reason")

    local unknown = SSA.compile_nodes({ { op = "MysteryNativeOp", source = 3, inputs = {}, outputs = {}, effect = "none" } }, {})
    assert_true(not unknown.ok, "unknown reopened node must reject")
    assert_eq((unknown.rejections or {})[1].reason, "unknown_reopened_node", "unknown node rejection reason")
end

-- Atoms reopen as typed semantic node specs, preserving graph-derived NF.
do
    local facts = field_facts(true)
    facts[#facts + 1] = Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R1"), "is_i64", true)
    local r = SSA.compile({
        { op = "GETFIELD", a = 1, b = 0, c = 2 },
        { op = "ADDI", a = 1, b = 1, c = 1 },
        { op = "SETFIELD", a = 0, b = 2, c = 1 },
    }, facts)
    local reopened = SSA.compile_nodes(r.active_node_specs, {})
    assert_true(reopened.ok, table.concat(reopened.errors or {}, "\n"))
    assert_true(has(reopened.stencil_ops, "store_i64_slot"), "atom reopen must preserve lowered store shape")
    assert_true(has(reopened.stencil_ops, "table_field_load"), "atom reopen must preserve field load shape")
end

print("ok - real SponJIT fact + SSA invariants")

}
  if include_barrier then xs[#xs + 1] = Facts.fact("gc", Facts.global_subject(), "barrier_clean", true) end
  return xs
end

local field_payload = {
  shape_offset = ffi.offsetof("SponTestTable", "shape_id"),
  shape_id = 0x1234abcd,
  metatable_offset = ffi.offsetof("SponTestTable", "metatable"),
  field_offset = ffi.offsetof("SponTestTable", "field0"),
  slots = { [0] = 1, [1] = 0, [2] = 2 },
}
local table_keepalive = {}

local getfield = lower_native({ { op = "GETFIELD", a = 1, b = 0, c = 2 } }, field_facts(false))
assert_true((function()
  local roles = {}
  for _, dr in ipairs(getfield.data_relocs or {}) do roles[dr.role_kind] = true end
  return roles.shape_offset and roles.shape_id and roles.metatable_offset and roles.field_offset
end)(), "GETFIELD must expose table payload relocs")
run_native(getfield,
  function(buf, f) patch_payloads(buf, f, field_payload) end,
  function(ctx, stack)
    local t = ffi.new("SponTestTable[1]")
    table_keepalive.getfield = t
    t[0].shape_id = field_payload.shape_id
    t[0].metatable = nil
    t[0].field0.value_ = 77
    t[0].field0.tt_ = 3
    stack[0].value_ = ptr_u64(t)
    stack[0].tt_ = 69 -- collectable LUA_VTABLE
    ctx.exit_kind = 99
  end,
  function(ctx, stack)
    assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_kind), 0, "GETFIELD exit")
    assert_eq(tonumber(stack[1].value_), 77, "GETFIELD value")
    assert_eq(tonumber(stack[1].tt_), 3, "GETFIELD tag")
  end)
run_native(getfield,
  function(buf, f) patch_payloads(buf, f, field_payload) end,
  function(ctx, stack)
    local t = ffi.new("SponTestTable[1]")
    table_keepalive.getfield_fail = t
    t[0].shape_id = 0
    t[0].metatable = nil
    t[0].field0.value_ = 77
    t[0].field0.tt_ = 3
    stack[0].value_ = ptr_u64(t)
    stack[0].tt_ = 69
    stack[1].value_ = 0
    stack[1].tt_ = 0
    ctx.exit_kind = 99
  end,
  function(ctx, stack)
    assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_kind), 1, "GETFIELD shape guard exit")
    assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_pc), 1, "GETFIELD guard pc")
    assert_eq(tonumber(stack[1].tt_), 0, "GETFIELD failed guard must not store")
  end)

local setfield = lower_native({ { op = "SETFIELD", a = 0, b = 2, c = 1 } }, field_facts(true))
local setfield_payload = {}
for k, v in pairs(field_payload) do setfield_payload[k] = v end
setfield_payload.slots = { [0] = 0, [1] = 1 }
run_native(setfield,
  function(buf, f) patch_payloads(buf, f, setfield_payload) end,
  function(ctx, stack)
    local t = ffi.new("SponTestTable[1]")
    table_keepalive.setfield = t
    ctx.scratch[0].value_ = ptr_u64(t)
    t[0].shape_id = field_payload.shape_id
    t[0].metatable = nil
    t[0].field0.value_ = 0
    t[0].field0.tt_ = 0
    stack[0].value_ = ptr_u64(t)
    stack[0].tt_ = 69
    stack[1].value_ = 88
    stack[1].tt_ = 3
    ctx.exit_kind = 99
  end,
  function(ctx)
    local t = ffi.cast("SponTestTable *", ffi.cast("uintptr_t", ctx.scratch[0].value_))
    assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_kind), 0, "SETFIELD exit")
    assert_eq(tonumber(t[0].field0.value_), 88, "SETFIELD value")
    assert_eq(tonumber(t[0].field0.tt_), 3, "SETFIELD tag")
  end)

local self_stencil = lower_native({ { op = "SELF", a = 1, b = 0, c = 2 } }, field_facts(false))
run_native(self_stencil,
  function(buf, f) patch_payloads(buf, f, field_payload) end,
  function(ctx, stack)
    local t = ffi.new("SponTestTable[1]")
    table_keepalive.self = t
    t[0].shape_id = field_payload.shape_id
    t[0].metatable = nil
    t[0].field0.value_ = 0xfeed
    t[0].field0.tt_ = 3
    stack[0].value_ = ptr_u64(t)
    stack[0].tt_ = 69
    ctx.exit_kind = 99
  end,
  function(ctx, stack)
    assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_kind), 0, "SELF exit")
    assert_eq(tonumber(stack[1].value_), 0xfeed, "SELF method value")
    assert_eq(tonumber(stack[1].tt_), 3, "SELF method tag")
    assert_eq(tonumber(stack[2].tt_), 69, "SELF receiver tag")
  end)

local unsupported = SSA.compile({ { op = "GETUPVAL", a = 0, b = 0 } }, {})
assert_true(not unsupported.ok, "GETUPVAL must remain explicit missing coverage")
local unsupported_table = SSA.compile({ { op = "GETTABLE", a = 0, b = 0, c = 1 } }, {})
assert_true(not unsupported_table.ok, "GETTABLE without table/array payload facts must remain explicit missing coverage")
local unsupported_pow = SSA.compile({ { op = "POW", a = 0, b = 0, c = 1 } }, { Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R0"), "is_i64", true), Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R1"), "is_i64", true) })
assert_true(not unsupported_pow.ok, "POW must remain unsupported until exact Lua pow semantics exist")

-- ssa.lua -- public facade for SpongeJIT semantic SSA + Stencil IR.
--
-- Offline foundry brain:
--   facts/opcodes -> semantic SSA -> Lua-semantic optimization ->
--   hole-parametric Stencil IR -> canonical stencil hash/form.
--
-- Runtime remains simple copy/patch/execute and never runs SSA/lowering.

local Facts = require("src.facts")
local Lift = require("src.ssa_lift")
local Opt = require("src.ssa_opt")
local Atoms = require("src.ssa_atoms")
local Validate = require("src.ssa_validate")
local Lower = require("src.ssa_to_stencil")
local StencilIR = require("src.stencil_ir")
local StencilNorm = require("src.stencil_normalize")

local M = {}

M.Facts = Facts
M.Validate = Validate
M.StencilIR = StencilIR
M.StencilNormalize = StencilNorm

local function factset(facts)
    if getmetatable(facts) == Facts.FactSet then return facts end
    return Facts.new(facts or {})
end

local function copy_array(xs)
    local out = {}
    for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
    return out
end

local function copy_map(t)
    local out = {}
    for k, v in pairs(t or {}) do out[k] = v end
    return out
end

local function budget_ok(g, config)
    config = config or {}
    local active = 0
    local gpr_pressure = 0
    for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
        if not n.removed then
            active = active + 1
            for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do
                local vv = g.values[v]
                if vv and vv.residency == "gpr0" then gpr_pressure = math.max(gpr_pressure, 1) end
            end
        end
    end
    local max_nodes = tonumber(config.max_ssa_nodes or 128) or 128
    local max_gpr = tonumber(config.max_live_gpr or 4) or 4
    return active <= max_nodes and gpr_pressure <= max_gpr
end

local function active_node_specs(g)
    local out = {}
    for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
        if not n.removed then
            local input_types, input_residencies = {}, {}
            for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do
                local vv = g.values[v]
                input_types[#input_types + 1] = vv and vv.ty or "Unknown"
                input_residencies[#input_residencies + 1] = vv and vv.residency or false
            end
            local output_types, output_residencies = {}, {}
            for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do
                local vv = g.values[v]
                output_types[#output_types + 1] = vv and vv.ty or "Unknown"
                output_residencies[#output_residencies + 1] = vv and vv.residency or false
            end
            out[#out + 1] = {
                op = n.op,
                codegen_op = n.codegen_op,
                args = n.args or {},
                effect = n.effect,
                guard_fact = n.guard and n.guard.fact,
                deps = n.deps or {},
                inputs = n.inputs or {},
                outputs = n.outputs or {},
                input_types = input_types,
                input_residencies = input_residencies,
                output_types = output_types,
                output_residencies = output_residencies,
                source = n.source,
                mem_in = copy_map(n.mem_in),
                mem_out = copy_map(n.mem_out),
                exit = n.exit and copy_map(n.exit) or nil,
            }
        end
    end
    return out
end

local function semantic_ops(g)
    local out = {}
    for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do if not n.removed then out[#out + 1] = n.op end end
    return out
end

local function rejection_errors(g)
    local out = {}
    for _, r in ipairs(g.rejections or {}) do
        out[#out + 1] = string.format("rejected %s at pc %s: %s", tostring(r.opcode), tostring(r.pc), tostring(r.reason))
    end
    return out
end

local function summarize(g, source_ops, facts, config)
    if g.rejected then
        local errors = rejection_errors(g)
        return {
            ok = false,
            errors = errors,
            rejections = copy_array(g.rejections),
            graph = g,
            factset = g.factset,
            stencil = nil,
            stencil_form = {},
            stencil_hash = nil,
            stencil_key = nil,
            stencil_ops = {},
            stencil_holes = {},
            slotmaps = {},
            active_node_specs = active_node_specs(g),
            semantic_ops = semantic_ops(g),
            checked_facts = {},
            checked_fact_objects = {},
            deps = {},
            projection = { ok = false, exit_obligations = 0, virtual_values = 0, reasons = {} },
            stats = g.stats,
            source_ops = copy_array(source_ops or {}),
        }
    end
    local ok_validate, errors = Validate.validate(g, config)
    local st = Lower.lower(g, source_ops or {}, config)
    local ok_stencil, st_errors = StencilIR.validate(st)
    errors = errors or {}
    for _, e in ipairs(st_errors or {}) do errors[#errors + 1] = e end
    local ok = ok_validate and ok_stencil and budget_ok(g, config)
    local form = StencilNorm.form(st)
    local key = StencilNorm.key(st)
    local hash = StencilNorm.hash(st)
    return {
        ok = ok,
        errors = errors,
        graph = g,
        factset = g.factset,
        stencil = st,
        stencil_form = form,
        stencil_hash = hash,
        stencil_key = key,
        stencil_ops = StencilNorm.active_codegen_ops(st),
        stencil_holes = st.holes,
        slotmaps = st.slotmaps,
        active_node_specs = active_node_specs(g),
        semantic_ops = semantic_ops(g),
        checked_facts = StencilNorm.checked_fact_names(st),
        checked_fact_objects = StencilNorm.checked_facts(st),
        deps = StencilNorm.deps(st),
        projection = StencilNorm.projection(st),
        stats = g.stats,
        source_ops = copy_array(source_ops or {}),
    }
end

function M.expand(ops, facts, config)
    return Lift.lift(ops, factset(facts), config)
end

function M.optimize(g, config)
    return Opt.optimize(g, config)
end

function M.compile(ops, facts, config)
    local fs = factset(facts)
    local g = Lift.lift(ops or {}, fs, config)
    if not g.rejected then Opt.optimize(g, config) end
    return summarize(g, ops or {}, fs, config)
end

function M.compile_nodes(node_ops, facts, config)
    local fs = factset(facts)
    local first = (node_ops or {})[1]
    assert(type(first) == "table" or first == nil, "compile_nodes accepts semantic node specs only; codegen-op reopening was removed with Stencil IR")
    local g = Atoms.reopen_node_specs(node_ops or {}, fs, config)
    if not g.rejected then Opt.optimize(g, config) end
    return summarize(g, node_ops or {}, fs, config)
end

return M

-- ssa_contract.lua -- stencil fact-transfer contracts from Stencil IR.
--
-- Contracts are over the same 64-bit ABI as runtime facts, but slot facts are
-- encoded in canonical stencil slot-class space. Generated stencil selector
-- code remaps these masks to actual runtime slots using stencil.slotmaps.

local Sig = require("src.fact_signature")
local Facts = require("src.facts")

local M = {}

local function copy_array(xs)
  local out = {}
  for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
  return out
end

local function slot_number_from_subject(subj)
  local id = subj and subj.id
  if type(id) == "number" then return id end
  if type(id) == "string" then return tonumber(id:match("^R(%d+)$") or id:match("^(%d+)$")) end
  return nil
end

local function remap_fact_to_canonical(f, st)
  if type(f) ~= "table" then return f end
  local slot = slot_number_from_subject(f.subject)
  if slot == nil then return f end
  local cls = st and st.slot_class_by_concrete and st.slot_class_by_concrete[slot]
  if cls == nil then return f end
  local nf = {}
  for k, v in pairs(f) do nf[k] = v end
  nf.subject = Facts.slot("R" .. tostring(cls))
  return nf
end

local function remap_facts(facts, st)
  local out = {}
  for _, f in ipairs(facts or {}) do out[#out + 1] = remap_fact_to_canonical(f, st) end
  return out
end

local function node_kills_all_vm_facts(n)
  if n.effect == "call" or n.op == "ExitBoundary" then return true end
  if n.effect == "heap_write" or n.op == "FieldStore" or n.op == "ArrayStore" then return true end
  if n.effect == "gc_barrier" or n.op == "BarrierCheck" then return true end
  return false
end

local function op_is_hard_exit(n)
  return n.op == "ExitBoundary"
end

function M.from_result(ssa_result, facts)
  local st = assert(ssa_result and ssa_result.stencil, "ssa_contract requires ssa_result.stencil")
  local selector = Sig.encode(remap_facts(facts or ssa_result.facts or {}, st))
  local checked = Sig.empty()
  local produced = Sig.empty()
  local killed = Sig.empty()
  local deps = {}
  local exits = {}

  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do
    if n.guard and n.guard.fact then
      checked = Sig.bor(checked, Sig.encode({remap_fact_to_canonical(n.guard.fact, st)}))
    end
    for _, d in ipairs(n.deps or {}) do deps[#deps + 1] = d end
    if n.exit then exits[#exits + 1] = {pc = n.source or 0, reason = n.exit.reason or "exit"} end

    if n.op == "StoreSlot" or n.op == "StoreI64Slot" then
      local slot = n.hole and n.hole.role_arg
      killed = Sig.bor(killed, Sig.slot_kill(slot))
      if n.op == "StoreI64Slot" then produced = Sig.bor(produced, Sig.i64_slot(slot)) end
    end

    if node_kills_all_vm_facts(n) then
      killed = Sig.bor(killed, Sig.all_table_and_payload_facts())
    end

    if op_is_hard_exit(n) then
      killed = Sig.bor(killed, Sig.all_slot_facts())
      killed = Sig.bor(killed, Sig.all_table_and_payload_facts())
    end
  end

  local required = Sig.minus(selector, checked)
  return {
    selector_sig = Sig.with_literal(selector),
    required_sig = Sig.with_literal(required),
    checked_sig = Sig.with_literal(checked),
    produced_sig = Sig.with_literal(produced),
    killed_sig = Sig.with_literal(killed),
    deps = copy_array(deps),
    exits = exits,
  }
end

return M

-- fact_signature.lua -- canonical 64-bit selector/contract fact encoding.
--
-- This is the single source for the compact runtime fact universe exported by
-- SponBank.  Facts in the rich foundry lattice are projected into this fixed
-- 64-bit ABI.  A TileTemplate may also carry transfer masks over the same
-- universe: required/checked/produced/killed.

local bit = require("bit")

local M = {}

M.SLOT_FACT_BASE = {
  i64 = 0, is_i64 = 0,
  table = 8, is_table = 8,
  shape_known = 16,
  metatable_absent = 24,
  array_hit = 32,
  bounds_ok = 40,
  known_call_target = 48,
  key_i64 = 0,
}

M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT = {
  barrier_clean = 56,
  const_i64 = 57,
  key_const = 58,
  nonzero_i64 = 59,
  shape_payload = 60,
  array_payload = 61,
  call_target_payload = 62,
}

local SLOT_FACT_BASES = {0, 8, 16, 24, 32, 40, 48}

function M.slot_fact_bases()
  local out = {}
  for i, b in ipairs(SLOT_FACT_BASES) do out[i] = b end
  return out
end

function M.global_payload_mask()
  return M.from_bits({56, 57, 58, 59, 60, 61, 62})
end

local function u32(x)
  x = tonumber(x) or 0
  if x < 0 then x = x + 4294967296 end
  return x
end
M.u32 = u32

local function hex32(n)
  n = u32(n or 0)
  local hi = math.floor(n / 65536) % 65536
  local lo = n % 65536
  return string.format("%04x%04x", hi, lo)
end

function M.literal(sig)
  sig = sig or M.empty()
  return "0x" .. hex32(sig.hi or 0) .. hex32(sig.lo or 0) .. "ULL"
end

function M.empty()
  return {lo = 0, hi = 0, pop = 0}
end

local function add_bit_mut(sig, bitpos)
  if not bitpos or bitpos < 0 or bitpos > 63 then return end
  sig._seen = sig._seen or {}
  if sig._seen[bitpos] then return end
  sig._seen[bitpos] = true
  sig.pop = (sig.pop or 0) + 1
  if bitpos < 32 then
    sig.lo = u32(bit.bor(sig.lo or 0, bit.lshift(1, bitpos)))
  else
    sig.hi = u32(bit.bor(sig.hi or 0, bit.lshift(1, bitpos - 32)))
  end
end

function M.from_bits(bits)
  local sig = M.empty()
  for _, bitpos in ipairs(bits or {}) do add_bit_mut(sig, bitpos) end
  sig._seen = nil
  return sig
end

function M.normalize(sig)
  sig = sig or M.empty()
  return {lo = u32(sig.lo or 0), hi = u32(sig.hi or 0), pop = sig.pop or M.popcount(sig)}
end

function M.popcount(sig)
  sig = sig or M.empty()
  local n, x = 0, u32(sig.lo or 0)
  while x ~= 0 do x = bit.band(x, x - 1); n = n + 1 end
  x = u32(sig.hi or 0)
  while x ~= 0 do x = bit.band(x, x - 1); n = n + 1 end
  return n
end

function M.bor(a, b)
  a, b = M.normalize(a), M.normalize(b)
  local out = {lo = u32(bit.bor(a.lo, b.lo)), hi = u32(bit.bor(a.hi, b.hi))}
  out.pop = M.popcount(out)
  return out
end

function M.band(a, b)
  a, b = M.normalize(a), M.normalize(b)
  local out = {lo = u32(bit.band(a.lo, b.lo)), hi = u32(bit.band(a.hi, b.hi))}
  out.pop = M.popcount(out)
  return out
end

function M.minus(a, b)
  a, b = M.normalize(a), M.normalize(b)
  local out = {lo = u32(bit.band(a.lo, bit.bnot(b.lo))), hi = u32(bit.band(a.hi, bit.bnot(b.hi)))}
  out.pop = M.popcount(out)
  return out
end

function M.is_empty(sig)
  sig = sig or M.empty()
  return u32(sig.lo or 0) == 0 and u32(sig.hi or 0) == 0
end

function M.parse_slot(subject)
  local id = subject and subject.id
  if type(id) == "number" then return id end
  if type(id) == "string" then return tonumber(id:match("^R(%d+)$") or id:match("^(%d+)$")) end
  return nil
end

function M.fact_bit(f)
  local pred = tostring(f and f.predicate or "")
  local subject = (f and f.subject) or {}
  local slot = M.parse_slot(subject)
  local base = M.SLOT_FACT_BASE[pred]
  if base and slot and slot >= 0 and slot < 8 then return base + slot end
  if pred == "barrier_clean" then return M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT.barrier_clean end
  if pred == "const_i64" then return M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT.const_i64 end
  if pred == "key_const" then return M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT.key_const end
  if pred == "nonzero_i64" then return M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT.nonzero_i64 end
  if pred == "shape_eq" or pred == "field_offset" then return M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT.shape_payload end
  if pred == "array_base_offset" then return M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT.array_payload end
  if pred == "target_eq" then return M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT.call_target_payload end
  return nil
end

function M.encode(facts)
  local bits = {}
  for _, f in ipairs(facts or {}) do
    local bitpos = M.fact_bit(f)
    if bitpos then bits[#bits + 1] = bitpos end
  end
  return M.from_bits(bits)
end

function M.with_literal(sig)
  sig = M.normalize(sig)
  sig.literal = M.literal(sig)
  return sig
end

function M.slot_kill(slot)
  slot = tonumber(slot)
  if not slot or slot < 0 or slot >= 8 then return M.empty() end
  local bits = {}
  for _, base in ipairs(SLOT_FACT_BASES) do bits[#bits + 1] = base + slot end
  return M.from_bits(bits)
end

function M.all_slot_facts()
  local out = M.empty()
  for slot = 0, 7 do out = M.bor(out, M.slot_kill(slot)) end
  return out
end

function M.all_table_and_payload_facts()
  local bits = {}
  for _, base in ipairs({8, 16, 24, 32, 40, 48}) do
    for slot = 0, 7 do bits[#bits + 1] = base + slot end
  end
  for _, bitpos in ipairs({56, 57, 58, 59, 60, 61, 62}) do bits[#bits + 1] = bitpos end
  return M.from_bits(bits)
end

function M.i64_slot(slot)
  slot = tonumber(slot)
  if not slot or slot < 0 or slot >= 8 then return M.empty() end
  return M.from_bits({M.SLOT_FACT_BASE.is_i64 + slot})
end

function M.remap_slots(sig, slot_map)
  sig = M.normalize(sig)
  slot_map = slot_map or {}
  local out = M.empty()
  for _, base in ipairs(SLOT_FACT_BASES) do
    for slot = 0, 7 do
      local bitpos = base + slot
      local word = bitpos < 32 and sig.lo or sig.hi
      local mask = bit.lshift(1, bitpos % 32)
      if bit.band(word, mask) ~= 0 then
        local dst = slot_map[slot]
        if dst == nil then dst = slot end
        dst = tonumber(dst)
        if dst and dst >= 0 and dst < 8 then add_bit_mut(out, base + dst) end
      end
    end
  end
  for _, bitpos in pairs(M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT) do
    local word = bitpos < 32 and sig.lo or sig.hi
    local mask = bit.lshift(1, bitpos % 32)
    if bit.band(word, mask) ~= 0 then add_bit_mut(out, bitpos) end
  end
  out._seen = nil
  return M.normalize(out)
end

return M

# SponJIT Architecture — Native Stencil Vocabulary + Online Fusion

**Status:** current design overview. The maintained implementation now produces executable native-stencil byte banks for the supported first surface. No copy/link/patch runtime is wired into the Lalin VM yet.

---

## One-sentence architecture

SponJIT builds a vocabulary of preverified native-stencil atoms offline, then Tier 2 performs long-range online fusion over those atoms to produce a `FusionPlan` that a mechanical copy/link/patch materializer can turn into one executable image.

```text
foundry/offline:
  bytecode/facts -> SSA -> Stencil IR -> abstract/native stencil atoms

Tier 2 online:
  hot atom span + facts -> FusionPlan

materializer/next:
  FusionPlan + NativeStencilBank -> LinkedImage
```

---

## Current implementation reality

The live maintained pipeline is now a native stencil-bank build:

```text
opcode sequence + fact bundle
→ semantic SSA consumes opcode semantics and facts
→ canonical Stencil IR normal form
→ global normal-form equivalence class
→ native x86-64 byte stencil for one representative
→ ABI-lowered descriptor metadata + byte bank
```

Key modules:

```text
spongejit/src/ssa_ir.lua
spongejit/src/ssa_lift.lua
spongejit/src/ssa_opt.lua
spongejit/src/ssa.lua
spongejit/src/ssa_atoms.lua
spongejit/src/stencil_ir.lua
spongejit/src/ssa_to_stencil.lua
spongejit/src/stencil_normalize.lua
spongejit/src/stencil_lower.lua
spongejit/src/stencil_desc.lua
spongejit/src/stencil_projection.lua
spongejit/src/worker_compile.lua
spongejit/src/dedupe_normal_forms.lua
```

Current chunked outputs from `spongejit/build_stencils.sh` / `make stencils`:

```text
grammar_chunk_N.json
grammar_worker_N.sqlite
stencil_bank.sqlite  -- unique normal forms, lowered stencils, pattern aliases
```

`make bank` consumes `stencil_bank.sqlite` and builds `build/sponbank/libsponbank.so`, including native byte bodies for supported stencils plus descriptor/selector arrays.

There is currently no maintained copy/link/patch runtime, native stencil linker, or Lalin VM integration.

---

## Vocabulary

### Atom

An **atom** is a precomputed stencil template in the bank vocabulary. It may come from any foundry layer: L0, L1, L2, L3, or later. The layer does not matter to Tier 2 once the atom is in the vocabulary.

### Stencil

A **native stencil** is a relocatable native-code region with explicit endpoints, data relocations, control relocations, fact transfer, clobbers, and projection metadata.

An **abstract stencil** is a non-executable descriptor for future analysis surfaces. Unsupported opcode/fact cases now reject before materialization rather than becoming executable fallback stubs.

### FusionPlan

A `FusionPlan` is the Tier 2 product: a long-range composition of atom instances plus explicit success edges, exit edges, fact flow, projection obligations, seam decisions, and materialization choices.

### LinkedImage

A `LinkedImage` is the runtime product: one executable image created by copying stencil bytes, patching data/control relocations, installing projection stubs, and publishing it atomically. The maintained tree now has a standalone Lua materializer smoke path for straight-line images; production publishing/VM integration is still future work.

---

## Phase tree

| Phase | Product | Current? |
|---|---|---|
| Foundry enumeration | opcode/fact atom candidates | yes |
| Semantic SSA | fact-specialized semantic graph | yes |
| Stencil IR | canonical hole-parametric shape | yes |
| Native stencil emission | executable stencil bytes + real relocs | yes, first surface |
| Tier 2 online fusion planner | `FusionPlan` over atoms | design / next work |
| Standalone copy/link/patch materializer | executable straight-line image | yes, first surface |
| Runtime image publisher / VM materializer | `LinkedImage` | future |
| Lalin VM integration | active image execution from `src/vm_loop.lua` | gated off |

---

## Foundry role

The foundry creates the atom vocabulary. It may do expensive work offline:

- enumerate local atom tuples;
- apply fact combinations;
- build semantic SSA;
- lower to Stencil IR;
- validate stencil descriptors;
- dedupe canonical normal forms after SSA consumes semantics;
- retain accepted native normal forms for later-layer composition.

The working saturation target is local:

```text
atom arity <= 4
~32 fact combinations per atom shape
```

This is not a Tier 2 trace limit. It is a vocabulary target.

---

## Tier 2 role

Tier 2 does long-range fusion over atom streams.

```text
4000 atoms
  -> online fusion planner
  -> one FusionPlan
  -> one LinkedImage after materialization
```

Tier 2 may optimize by choosing among prebuilt atoms, variants, endpoints, bridges, and projection stubs:

- direct atom-to-atom continuations;
- hot fallthrough layout;
- bridge insertion or omission;
- store/load seam removal;
- box/unbox seam removal;
- redundant guard removal;
- projection-stub sharing;
- cold exit outlining;
- dependency-aware variant choice.

Tier 2 must not synthesize arbitrary native instructions. It optimizes the stencil graph, not machine code.

---

## Why granular atoms are enough

Small atoms give the fusion planner flexibility. If the bank has atom variants with precise contracts, a long fused image can be trace-like without a runtime code generator.

The required contracts are:

- input/output locations;
- clobbers;
- fact transfer;
- data relocations;
- control relocations;
- projection recipes;
- dependency obligations;
- endpoint compatibility.

If atoms only expose frame-synced boundaries, Tier 2 mainly removes dispatch/linking overhead. If atoms expose register/value-flow endpoints and reconstructing projections, Tier 2 can remove frame seams and behave much more like a trace optimizer.

---

## Runtime role

The future runtime is deliberately simple compared with a tracing compiler:

```text
observe stable hot span
  -> invoke online fusion planner over bank atoms
  -> materialize chosen FusionPlan
  -> publish active image
  -> on exit, project state and fall back/demote/replan
```

The runtime does not run SSA, invent new instructions, infer projection recipes, or patch live images in place.

---

## Floor and active image

When executable SponJIT exists, each enabled region has:

```text
floor   always-correct native baseline image
active  current selected image; may equal floor
```

The floor requires exact semantic native coverage for the enabled opcode subset, and missing coverage is reported explicitly. Until such a floor exists, SponJIT remains a foundry/planner prototype and the Lalin VM uses its normal interpreter path.

---

## Relation to Lalin VM

The Lalin interpreter VM in `src/` is not JIT-integrated today. `src/contract.lua` keeps:

```lua
sponjit_allowed = false
```

SponJIT must not become an execution engine for `src/vm_loop.lua` until VM gates around bytecode validation, frame/cache semantics, native ABI, allocation, errors, and coroutine/yield behavior are satisfied.

---

## Canonical documents

| Document | Role |
|---|---|
| `SPONJIT_FOUNDRY_SSA.md` | Foundry/SSA atom vocabulary design. |
| `SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md` | Online fusion planner data/control spec. |
| `SPONJIT_COPY_LINK_PATCH.md` | Native stencil ABI and copy-link-patch conformance. |
| `SPONJIT_RUNTIME_DESIGN.md` | Future runtime/materializer design. |
| `README.md` | Directory status and test commands. |

---

## Non-goals

SponJIT does not currently:

- execute native stencil images;
- integrate with the Lalin VM;
- ship an executable stencil bank;
- maintain a runtime code cache;
- run a LuaJIT-style IR optimizer/emitter at runtime;
- rely on C-function-shaped stencils as a conforming backend.

---

## One-line rule

```text
The foundry creates atoms.
Tier 2 chooses and proves a long fusion over atoms.
The materializer copies, links, patches, and publishes exactly that proof.
```

# SponJIT Copy-Link-Patch Native Stencil ABI Specification

**Status:** draft specification; not implemented.

**Spec version:** 0.1

**Decision:** Copy-Link-Patch uses a **Native Stencil ABI with Continuation
Relocations**. C-function-shaped stencils are not linkable stencils.

The keywords **MUST**, **MUST NOT**, **SHOULD**, **SHOULD NOT**, and **MAY** are
normative.

---

## 1. Scope

This specification defines the ABI for SpongeJIT Copy-Link-Patch stencils.

It specifies:

- native stencil descriptors
- physical endpoint contracts
- data relocations
- control relocations
- continuation endpoints
- projection recipes
- validation rules
- image linking rules
- rejection conditions

It does not specify:

- a general machine-code assembler
- a full optimizing compiler
- a Lalin/Cranelift backend
- payload lease policy for all future object-model facts
- final binary encoding of descriptor arrays

---

## 2. Unified tier model

There is one target stencil ABI: native stencils.

C-function-shaped tiles are an old implementation artifact, not a second target ABI.
A conforming SpongeJIT bank MUST expose exact semantic native stencils for the
implemented floor subset and hot fused images. Missing opcode/fact coverage is a
compile/lower/select rejection, not a generated fallback stub.

### 2.1 Floor / Tier 1 image

The floor/Tier 1 image uses the same native stencil ABI as every hotter image.

Tier 1 behavior is the simplest legal linking policy:

```text
select stencil templates -> canonical slot endpoints -> copy bytes -> patch data/control relocations -> enter image
```

Tier 1 SHOULD keep boundaries frame-synchronized unless projection recipes prove a
more direct endpoint is valid.

Tier 1 MUST NOT require a C-function return boundary for normal success flow.

### 2.2 Hot fused image

A hot fused image uses the same stencil descriptors and materializer, but is chosen by the Tier 2 online fusion planner. The planner may select longer spans, choose stencil variants, preserve register endpoints across stencils, insert or omit bridge stencils, prove seam removal, skip redundant guards through fact flow, and use richer projection recipes.

Hot fused image behavior is:

```text
observe stable hot span
  -> online fusion planner chooses/proves a FusionPlan from bank stencils
    -> materializer lays out one image
      -> patch data/control relocations
        -> enter once
```

A hot fused image MAY cover many more atoms than any single precomputed stencil. The atoms may come from L1, L2, L3, or later bank layers; the layer does not matter to Tier 2. A 4000-atom hot span can be materialized as one linked image by composing many atom instances. Atom arity/layer limits the size of individual templates, not the length of the fused image.

The initial bank target is local arity <= 4 with roughly 32 fact combinations per atom shape after normal-form dedupe. This is a vocabulary target, not a trace-length target. Tier 2 obtains long-range optimization by composing many such atoms and choosing variants/endpoints/projections across the whole fused span.

No tier MAY synthesize arbitrary machine instructions at runtime.

All tiers MAY copy, lay out, link, and patch only descriptors emitted by the foundry. The online fusion planner may optimize the stencil graph; the linker/materializer only realizes that chosen graph mechanically.

---

## 3. Terms

### Stencil

A **stencil** is a relocatable native code region with explicit entry and exit
endpoints.

A stencil is not a C function.

### Endpoint

An **endpoint** is a named control position inside a stencil:

- `ENTRY`
- `OK`
- `GUARD_FAIL`
- `RESIDUAL`
- `BOUNDARY`
- `RETURN`
- `BRANCH_TRUE`
- `BRANCH_FALSE`

### Data relocation

A **data relocation** patches a value consumed by native code: slot offset,
immediate, constant, dependency address, payload address, etc.

### Control relocation

A **control relocation** patches a control-transfer target: success continuation,
guard failure target, VM boundary target, branch target, projection stub, return target.

### Projection

A **projection** reconstructs interpreter-visible state at a non-success exit.

### Physical ABI

A **physical ABI** defines architecture, platform calling convention, fixed register
roles, clobber rules, stack rules, and context/frame locations.

---

## 4. Non-goals and prohibitions

A Copy-Link-Patch implementation MUST NOT treat existing `exit`/`fail` metadata
holes as native branch targets unless they are retyped as control relocations.

A Copy-Link-Patch implementation MUST NOT delete a frame store across an exit unless
that exit has a complete projection recipe.

A Copy-Link-Patch implementation MUST NOT patch unsupported hole roles with dummy
values.

A Copy-Link-Patch implementation MUST NOT infer clobbers or endpoint locations by
reverse-engineering machine code.

A Copy-Link-Patch implementation MUST NOT require runtime instruction encoding for
ordinary stencil linking.

---

## 5. Descriptor model

A stencil catalog MUST provide these descriptor classes:

```text
SponPhysicalAbiDesc
SponStencilDesc
SponEndpointDesc
SponLocationDesc
SponDataRelocDesc
SponControlRelocDesc
SponFactTransferDesc
SponEffectDesc
SponProjectionDesc
SponProjectionEntryDesc
SponDependencyDesc
```

The binary representation is implementation-defined, but the semantic fields in
this specification are mandatory unless explicitly marked optional.

---

## 6. Physical ABI descriptor

### 6.1 Required fields

```text
SponPhysicalAbiDesc:
  abi_id:              u16
  arch:                enum
  platform_abi:        enum
  pointer_width:        u8
  endian:              enum
  ctx_location:         Location
  frame_location:       Location
  scratch_locations:    LocationSet
  preserved_locations:  LocationSet
  flag_clobber_policy:  enum
  stack_alignment:      u16
  red_zone_policy:      enum
```

### 6.2 Rules

Each stencil MUST reference exactly one `SponPhysicalAbiDesc`.

The linker MUST reject a stencil whose `abi_id` is unsupported by the current
runtime.

The linker MUST verify that all linked stencils in one fused image use compatible
physical ABIs.

`ctx_location` and `frame_location` MUST be valid at every stencil entry.

A stencil MUST declare all clobbered physical locations that may be observed by
successor stencils or projection stubs.

---

## 7. Location model

### 7.1 Location kinds

```text
LOC_NONE
LOC_FRAME_SLOT
LOC_CANON_SLOT
LOC_GPR
LOC_XMM
LOC_FLAGS
LOC_IMM32
LOC_IMM64
LOC_CONST_POOL
LOC_CTX
LOC_FRAME_BASE
LOC_HELPER
```

### 7.2 Location descriptor

```text
SponLocationDesc:
  kind:      LocationKind
  index:     u16
  type:      ValueType
  flags:     LocationFlags
```

### 7.3 Value types

```text
VAL_UNKNOWN
VAL_TVALUE
VAL_BOOL
VAL_I64
VAL_F64
VAL_PTR
VAL_TABLE
VAL_SHAPE
VAL_CONTINUATION
VAL_VOID
```

### 7.4 Rules

A physical register location MUST name a concrete register class/member under the
stencil's `physical_abi_id`.

A stencil output location MUST NOT be assumed live after a successor stencil

# SponJIT Foundry SSA — Atom Vocabulary Design

**Status:** current design/implementation notes for the maintained native-stencil pipeline. The foundry emits SSA-consumed normal forms and lowers supported classes to executable x86-64 native stencil bytes plus explicit relocation metadata.

---

## One-sentence purpose

The SponJIT foundry enumerates local atom candidates, specializes them with explicit facts through semantic SSA, lowers useful candidates to Stencil IR and native stencil descriptors/bytes, and builds the vocabulary that Tier 2 later fuses over long hot spans.

---

## Current pipeline

The maintained pipeline is:

```text
opcode sequence + fact bundle
→ semantic SSA consumes opcode semantics and facts
→ canonical Stencil IR normal form
→ global normal-form equivalence class
→ lower one representative per supported class to native x86-64 stencil bytes
→ ABI-lowered descriptor metadata + byte bank
```

Equivalence class key for grammar/foundry mode:

```text
canonical_stencil_hash
```

The source opcode sequence and fact-transfer contract are **not** part of the normal-form key. Many opcode/fact candidates may collapse to one final form after SSA. Their source patterns are retained as aliases for selection; the stencil body/descriptor is emitted once.

## Loop boundary stance

`FORPREP`/`FORLOOP` and `TFOR*` are not scalar opcodes for the current fact-axis pass. They are structural loop-region boundaries. Until SpongeJIT has paired loop-region lowering with explicit loop edges, full loop-window slotmaps, topology bindings, and edge-sensitive fact transfer, the foundry must not advertise i64 loop fact bundles. Numeric and generic loops therefore lower only to exact VM boundary handoffs, not partially specialized stencils.

Important modules:

```text
spongejit/src/grammar_enum.lua       -- grammar / sequence enumeration
spongejit/src/enumerate.lua          -- corpus/window enumeration
spongejit/src/loop_regions.lua       -- paired numeric/generic loop topology records
spongejit/src/ssa_lift.lua           -- opcode/facts -> semantic SSA
spongejit/src/ssa_ir.lua             -- SSA graph/value/effect model
spongejit/src/ssa_opt.lua            -- local semantic cleanup
spongejit/src/ssa.lua                -- public compile facade
spongejit/src/ssa_atoms.lua          -- reopen serialized node specs
spongejit/src/stencil_ir.lua         -- canonical hole-parametric shape
spongejit/src/ssa_to_stencil.lua     -- SSA -> Stencil IR
spongejit/src/stencil_normalize.lua  -- canonical key/hash/form
spongejit/src/stencil_lower.lua       -- Stencil IR -> native stencil descriptor
spongejit/src/stencil_native_x64.lua  -- first-surface x86-64 byte emitter
spongejit/src/stencil_desc.lua        -- stencil schema, validation, ABI lowering
spongejit/src/worker_compile.lua     -- worker SQLite metadata artifacts
spongejit/src/dedupe_normal_forms.lua   -- global normal-form dedupe + unique lowering
```

Chunked metadata-bank build:

```sh
cd experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit
make stencils        # CHUNKS=128, WORKERS=$(nproc) by default
make bank             # libsponbank.so with native bytes + descriptors from stencil_bank.sqlite
```

`CHUNKS` and `WORKERS` are separate controls. More chunks reduce per-worker shard size; more workers increase parallelism.

SQLite outputs:

```text
grammar_chunk_N.json
grammar_worker_N.sqlite
stencil_bank.sqlite  -- unique normal forms, lowered stencils, pattern aliases
```

Bank outputs:

```text
build/sponbank/libsponbank.so
build/sponbank/sponbank_build_manifest.json
```

The generated shared library contains native stencil byte bodies for supported first-surface classes, plus endpoint, projection, slotmap, data-reloc, control-reloc, and selector metadata. It is still not a full runtime: the online fusion planner and copy/link/patch materializer remain separate work.

---

## Foundry vs Tier 2

The foundry builds local vocabulary. Tier 2 builds long-range fusions.

```text
Foundry:
  generate accepted native atoms and variants

Tier 2 online fusion planner:
  compose many atoms into one FusionPlan
```

A local atom may come from any foundry layer: L0, L1, L2, L3, or later. Tier 2 does not care which layer produced it; it sees a stream of atom candidates with explicit contracts.

---

## Atom vocabulary target

The current saturation target is local:

```text
atom arity <= 4
~32 fact combinations per atom shape
```

This is enough material to test the Tier 2 thesis because long traces are not precomputed as single giant atoms. Tier 2 composes many local atoms:

```text
4000 atom hot span
  -> FusionPlan over 4000 atom instances
  -> one future LinkedImage
```

The arity/fact-combo cap shapes the vocabulary. It does not cap the Tier 2 optimization range.

---

## Layer growth model

The foundry basis grows by accepting valid native atoms from local compositions:

```text
L0 = raw opcode atoms
for layer k:
  basis = union(L0..Lk)
  candidates = local tuples over basis, arity <= 4
  for candidate in candidates:
    for fact_env in fact combinations:
      ssa = specialize_with_facts(candidate, fact_env)
      stencil = lower_to_stencil(ssa)
      stencil = lower_to_native_stencil(stencil)
      accept iff semantic SSA, normal form validation, and native lowering all pass
  L{k+1} = unique accepted native normal forms from this layer
```

Rejected candidates are measurements, not runtime artifacts.

---

## Semantic SSA role

Semantic SSA is the fact-consuming layer.

It is responsible for:

- preserving opcode semantics;
- making fact consequences explicit;
- modeling effects, memory tokens, guards, exits, dependencies, and value residencies;
- exposing source PC and exit metadata;
- producing a graph suitable for Stencil IR lowering.

It is not a runtime IR. The future runtime must not build or optimize SSA.

---

## Stencil IR role

Stencil IR is the canonical materialization shape between semantic SSA and stencil descriptors.

It represents:

- stencil ops;
- values;
- data holes;
- slotmaps;
- facts;
- exits;
- source ops;
- effect/projection-relevant metadata.

Important invariant:

```text
exit/fail are not data holes
```

Exits are endpoint/projection/control concepts.

---

## Stencil lowering role

`stencil_lower.lua` lowers Stencil IR into native stencil descriptors and invokes the first-surface x86-64 byte emitter.

Current first surface:

```text
i64 slot loads/stores
i64 guards
unbox/box-relevant i64 operations
simple constants/immediates
simple exact boundary exits
```

Supported simple data roles:

```text
slot
slot_store
imm
const
bool
```

Payload-heavy roles require explicit payload leases and dependency invalidation before executable use.

---

## Output atom contract

An atom must eventually expose enough structure for Tier 2 fusion:

```text
AtomTemplate:
  semantic identity
  source span / layer identity
  fact transfer
  endpoint contracts
  input/output locations
  clobbers
  data relocations
  control relocations
  projection recipes
  dependency obligations
  cost metadata
  abstract or native body phase
```

If endpoint and projection contracts are too weak, Tier 2 cannot do meaningful seam optimization even with many atom variants.

---

## Fact combinations

The working target is roughly 32 fact combinations per atom shape.

Facts are not just one bitmask in the design model:

```text
FactState = signature bits + payload leases + dependency obligations
```

The current simple surface is mostly signature-bit based. Shape, field, call-target, barrier, and dependency-heavy facts must not be smuggled through dummy values.

---

## Relationship to Tier 2 online fusion

Tier 2 needs atom variants that expose choices:

```text
frame-synced endpoint variant
register-preserving endpoint variant

# SponJIT Runtime Design — Native Stencil Image Materialization

**Type:** future runtime/materializer specification  
**Methodology:** explicit programming  
**Status:** runtime/materializer design; native byte bank exists for the first surface, copy/link/patch runtime not implemented  
**Depends on:** `SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md` and `SPONJIT_COPY_LINK_PATCH.md`

---

## 0. Current reality

The maintained SponJIT implementation currently has a **native byte stencil bank** for the supported first surface:

```text
opcode sequence + fact bundle
→ semantic SSA
→ canonical Stencil IR normal form
→ global normal-form equivalence class
→ native x86-64 stencil bytes for one representative
→ grammar_worker_N.sqlite → stencil_bank.sqlite → libsponbank.so
```

There is currently no maintained copy/link/patch SponJIT runtime:

- no C-function tile bank;
- no maintained PUC runtime integration;
- no production native stencil linker/runtime publisher;
- only a standalone Lua copy/link/patch smoke materializer for straight-line first-surface images;
- no Lalin VM integration.

The Lalin interpreter VM gate remains closed:

```lua
sponjit_allowed = false
```

This document is therefore not a description of a working runtime. It is the runtime design that consumes the proof-bearing native stencil bank once the online fusion planner and materializer are implemented.

---

## 1. One-sentence purpose

The SponJIT runtime observes hot spans, invokes an online fusion planner over preverified native stencil templates, materializes the chosen fusion plan by copying bytes and patching data/control relocations, publishes that image atomically, and falls back through projection-safe exits without running arbitrary SSA optimization, register allocation, or code generation at runtime.

That sentence is the boundary:

```text
runtime may:       observe, plan fusion over bank stencils, select, copy, link, patch, publish, demote, log
runtime may not:   compile arbitrary code, synthesize instructions, infer hidden semantics, execute abstract stencils
```

---

## 2. Relationship to the other SponJIT specs

### 2.1 Online fusion planner spec

`SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md` defines the planner used in two binding-time modes.

Planner input mode:

```text
native stencil bank + observed facts + site memory
→ FusionPlan / FloorOnly / RejectionReport / Deferral
```

The runtime does not run a general compiler. It may run the online fusion planner over prebuilt native stencil templates. That planner can choose variants, bridges, endpoints, seams, and layout policies from the bank vocabulary; the materializer then realizes the chosen plan mechanically.

### 2.2 Native stencil ABI spec

`SPONJIT_COPY_LINK_PATCH.md` defines the future native stencil ABI:

```text
native bytes + endpoint metadata + data relocs + control relocs + projection recipes
```

This runtime design is the consumer of that ABI.

### 2.3 Runtime design boundary

This document starts at the first point where the system has a conforming native stencil bank:

```text
NativeStencilBank
  -> online fusion planning
  -> FusionPlan
  -> MaterializationPlan
  -> LinkedImage
  -> active execution
```

If a selected artifact is abstract rather than native, runtime materialization must return:

```text
Deferred(NeedsNativeStencils)
```

---

## 3. Retired terminology and replacement terminology

The old documents used `TileTemplate`, C stencils, and C-function-shaped tiles. That terminology is retired for maintained SponJIT runtime design.

Use these terms instead:

| Old term | New term | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| TileTemplate | NativeStencilTemplate | Relocatable native code region with explicit endpoints. |
| tile image | LinkedImage | Executable region image built from stencil instances. |
| tile exit | StencilExit | Non-success endpoint reached from native stencil code. |
| tile-site memory | StencilSiteMemory | Per-span selector memory for exits, penalties, cooldowns. |
| C stencil | non-conforming artifact | C functions are not linkable native stencils. |

`L0` remains as a layer concept, but it means exact semantic native stencils for the implemented floor subset. Missing opcode coverage is reported explicitly; it is not hidden behind fallback stubs.

---

## 4. Runtime thesis

SponJIT is not a tracing JIT in the LuaJIT sense: it does not record IR and emit arbitrary native code at runtime. Tier 2 can still behave like a long-range trace optimizer by optimizing a hot span as a **stencil graph** over prebuilt native stencils.

```text
LuaJIT-like system:
  runtime observes -> records -> optimizes IR -> emits native code -> caches traces

SponJIT:
  foundry builds a verified native stencil vocabulary offline
  runtime observes a hot span -> online fusion planner chooses a legal optimized stencil graph
  materializer copy/link/patches that plan -> publishes image
```

The bank is immutable. The runtime builds images from the bank. The runtime never creates a new code shape that was not already present as a native stencil template.

The central bet is:

```text
copy/link/patch is cheap enough that runtime adaptation can be
stencil-graph fusion, not live machine-code compilation.
```

### 4.1 Atom arity is not Tier 2 trace length

The foundry may precompute native stencil templates as atoms from any bank layer: L1, L2, L3, or later layers. An atom may itself represent a fused phrase over lower-layer atoms. That does **not** cap Tier 2 image length.

Tier 2 does long-range fusion over those atoms, whatever layer they come from:

```text
4000-atom hot span
  -> FusionPlan(atom_instance_1, atom_instance_2, ..., atom_instance_N)
  -> one LinkedImage
  -> enter once, run direct continuations, exit through projection stubs
```

The layer/arity of one atom describes the size of an individual template. Tier 2 optimization range is the length of the atom trace it chooses to fuse.

### 4.2 Initial bank saturation assumption

The current design target is a granular atom vocabulary: saturate atom generation to local arity 4 and keep roughly 32 fact combinations per atom shape after canonicalization and dedupe.

This is a local vocabulary assumption, not a Tier 2 trace limit:

```text
arity <= 4 per atom
~32 fact combos per atom shape
many atoms per FusionPlan
one linked image per hot span
```

That vocabulary is only valuable if atom descriptors expose value-flow structure: endpoint locations, fact transfer, clobbers, projection recipes, bridge compatibility, and variants that avoid forced frame synchronization. Without those contracts, Tier 2 gets mostly direct-linking wins. With them, Tier 2 can behave like a trace optimizer assembled from prebuilt atoms.

---

## 5. Runtime phase tree

The runtime phase tree is explicit:

| Phase | Input | Product | May fail as |
|---|---|---|---|
| Observation | active/floor execution events | `RuntimeObservation` | never; can be empty |
| Signature canonicalization | observations + fact schema | `ObservedSignature` | `UnsupportedObservation` |
| Candidate enumeration | bank + signature + site memory | `CandidateSet` | `NoCandidate`, then floor candidates |
| Online fusion planning | candidates + facts + endpoints + projections | `FusionPlan` / `StencilCover` | `FloorOnly`, `Deferred`, `Rejected` |
| Materialization planning | fusion plan + bank metadata | `MaterializationPlan` | `Deferred`, `Rejected` |
| Image building | materialization plan | writable `ImageBuild` | allocation/reloc failure |
| Patching | image build + runtime values | patched writable image | patch failure |
| Permission install | patched image | executable image | platform failure |
| Publication | executable image | active pointer update | never publishes partial image |
| Exit handling | stencil exit | projected fallback resume | projection failure is fatal/debug-only bug |
| Invalidation | dependency epoch advance | active demotion to floor | never continues stale image |

The runtime never jumps from fusion planning directly to execution. Every executable image passes through materialization and publication.

---

## 6. Runtime data tree

The runtime data tree is the semantic shape. C structs may lower these types, but the wire layout is not the design root.

### 6.1 Runtime state

```text
SponRuntime = {
  bank: NativeStencilBank,
  regions: RegionRuntimeSet,
  epochs: DependencyEpochTable,
  exit_log: ExitLog,
  policy: RuntimePolicy,
}
```

### 6.2 Region runtime state

```text
RegionRuntime = {
  region_id: RegionId,
  pc_start: SourcePc,
  pc_end: SourcePc,

  floor: LinkedImage,
  active: LinkedImage,

  observation: ObservationState,
  hyst: HysteresisState,
  sites: StencilSiteMemorySet,
  generation: ImageGeneration,
}
```

Rules:

- `floor` is never freed while the region exists.
- `active` may equal `floor`.
- `active` is the only adaptive executable pointer.
- the runtime does not maintain a trace/code cache.

```text

  abi: PhysicalAbiDesc,

-- util.lua
-- Shared helpers for the Lua VM JIT harness.

local M = {}

local cjson = require("cjson")
if cjson.encode_keep_buffer then cjson.encode_keep_buffer(false) end
if cjson.encode_empty_table_as_object then cjson.encode_empty_table_as_object(false) end

local bit_ok, bit = pcall(require, "bit")

function M.shell_quote(s)
    s = tostring(s)
    return "'" .. s:gsub("'", [['"'"']]) .. "'"
end

function M.path_exists(path)
    local f = io.open(path, "rb")
    if f then f:close(); return true end
    return false
end

function M.read_file(path)
    local f, err = io.open(path, "rb")
    if not f then return nil, err end
    local data = f:read("*a")
    f:close()
    return data
end

function M.write_file(path, data)
    local f, err = io.open(path, "wb")
    if not f then return false, err end
    f:write(data or "")
    f:close()
    return true
end
